concerns.text: Plan states '9988 prime knots (crossing number ≤13 per OEIS A002863)' — this is the count AT crossing number 13, not cumulative through 13. Cumulative count through c=13 is approximately 12,965. This factual error affects dataset scope validation and completeness claims (SC-001).

Composite complexity score constructed from crossing number and braid index, then validated against hyperbolic volume. While not circular (hyperbolic volume is geometrically distinct), this validation is redundant with regression analysis testing the same predictor-outcome relationship. Should clarify what unique insight composite score provides beyond the regression models.

ANOVA applied to discrete integer-valued invariants (crossing number, braid number are small integers). Plan acknowledges assumption violations and uses robust alternatives, but with N≈13,000 knots, normality assumptions may be less critical. Should justify why ANOVA is appropriate vs. non-parametric group comparison from the start.

Filtering to knots with valid hyperbolic volume excludes torus/satellite knots, creating selection bias. The analysis will only characterize hyperbolic knots, but conclusions may be over-generalized. Should explicitly limit scope to 'hyperbolic prime knots' rather than 'prime knots' in final reports.

VIF threshold of 5 is appropriate for multicollinearity assessment, but with braid index ≤ crossing number (known inequality), perfect multicollinearity may exist for some knots. Should verify VIF computation handles this edge case and document expected VIF range given the mathematical constraint.
